title: gastrodia elata var. viridis
intro: Gastrodia elata var. viridis is a rare and lesser-known variant of the Gastrodia elata plant, a type of orchid native to Asia, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and fleshy rhizomes. The medicinal properties of Gastrodia elata var. viridis include anti-inflammatory, anticonvulsant, and antispasmodic effects, which contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The plant has been used to alleviate symptoms of epilepsy, insomnia, and anxiety, as well as to treat digestive disorders and improve cognitive function. In herbal preparations, Gastrodia elata var. viridis is often used in combination with other herbs, such as Pueraria and Angelica, to create tonics and teas that promote relaxation and overall well-being.
